mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration that expands aggregate demand, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income combined with expanding consumer credit utilization indicates strong household purchasing power that directly fuels corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistically significant difference between high and low signal periods (p > 0.10), or the signal's predictive power decays to zero after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which lowers unit costs and boosts corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or INDPRO growth decouples from corporate earnings growth to the extent that forward returns turn negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, tight monetary conditions increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the rate level shows no statistically significant relationship with forward equity returns after adjusting for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth occurring alongside accelerating consumer price inflation signals a wage-price spiral that erodes real profit margins and triggers central bank tightening, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of observations, or CPIAUCSL and PAYEMS diverge without causing equity drawdowns over the 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts during a post-shock recovery environment signals rebuilding confidence and wealth effects that stimulate durable goods consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to lead equity returns by more than 2 trading days, or the signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of post-recession episodes over a 10-year test period.
